senses.gloss: teacher, instructor, schoolteacher, tutor, schoolmaster, pedagogue, educator
navigator, a kind of compliance officer on a ship, who checks that the technical stuff is in order on the vessel
bearer of news, notifier, informer, informant

etymology.text:
Derived from the passive participle of the verb عَلَّمَ (ʕallama, “to teach”).

Derived from the active participle of the verb عَلَّمَ (ʕallama, “to teach”).

Noun of place from the verb عَلِمَ (ʕalima, “to know, to perceive, to distinguish”).

Derived from the active participle of the verb أَعْلَمَ (ʔaʕlama, “to inform”).

body.text: [act. part. n. of 2, in all its senses: and generally meaning]
(KL.) ―         -b2-  [It is now also a common title of address to a Christian and to a Jew.]
